item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Vivid imagery, but no real plot or hook. It's a god that demands sacrifice, but it doesn't do anything more with that concept. Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it's always annoyed me that this thing is classified as safe when it should so odviously be Euclid. the reasoning that it's safe because it's predictable to contain makes absolutely no sense. by that logic 173 should be safe too. safe means put it in a box and forget about it. 005 is safe. 500 is safe. but most scps that take less effort to contain than this but more effort to than the examples I gave are almost universally considered Euclid. 1171 requires a researcher to live in its house and it's considered Euclid. 173 requires bi weekly cleaning and its also Euclid. I would *maybe* allow it if instead of literal human sacrifice once a month it was something much simpler like reading it something, but even then that's pushing it considering the consequences of not following its containment procedures. the foundation does not have infinite d classes.
odviously a - 1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If they’re certain that the effect has a range limit, then the Foundation should simply contain it within a large enough area. If they’re not, then this thing fails the Safe test spectacularly. “It’s Safe, and we’re containing it with active human sacrifice“ is wildly dissonant.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great anomaly, not only is it eerie and depressing, it's intriguing all around.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed to say "approximately 500 years". Thanks for the info!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Church records imply that SCP-2121 had been in the area for at least 1000 years, since the time of the founding of the village and the eradication of local pagan tribes.

User Den1s pointed out that as of today there's been some 1030 years since the baptism of Rus and less than 500 since the conquest of Siberia started. So it looks just as plausible as a Muslim settlement in the Aztec Empire.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like a few others, i also dont see how the Foundation didnt just make a fence around a 1.7 kilometers area. It sounds like a waste of D-Personel, even for the Foundation.

So that could definitely be polishd somehow, and find a new thing it does through starvation, that perhaps extends the area indefinitely untill satisfied? Until then;

-1 From me

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: Conwell made the edits

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP mentions ties to the "Horizon Initiative". However, the HI doesn't have an official hub yet. I propose linking to the tale Fire on the Horizon in lieu of this.

Editing this:

SCP-2121 was initially brought to the attention of the Foundation by representatives of the Horizon Initiative, who stated that due to its specific properties and the nature of their organization, they were unable to either destroy or effectively contain it.

To this:

SCP-2121 was initially brought to the attention of the Foundation by representatives of the Horizon Initiative, who stated that due to its specific properties and the nature of their organization, they were unable to either destroy or effectively contain it.

item.title: Re: Euclid-keter
item.content:encoded: Don't change someone else's SCP without their permission (or that of Staff). I made it Safe for a reason, which I've explained elsewhere in comments.

item.title: Re: Euclid-keter
item.content:encoded: Object Classes are determined by how easy/hard it is to be contained/how easy/hard it is to operate their containment procedures, not by how powerful it is. This is safe because all you have to do is hang a single D-class once every month.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah right

item.title: Euclid-keter
item.content:encoded: I think a god that hangs people should be classified at least euclid

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You hooked me in with the SCProcedures, but you did nothing with it. The body/religion horror does very little for me, and the skip ends leaving me with very little.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like it's sorta incomplete. maybe add an interview or 2, and give it more of a mystery. Still like it, though. +1

Also, found some fanart of it:
+ Fanart
- By munimu
[ I'm not to sure what the girl has to do with it, though.]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not really sure why D-Class are being fed to this thing. If it caused really bad effects if left alone, so be it - but all it does is loudly telepathically scream and make religious people suicide.

You could contain it more easily just by finding a 1.7km radius bit of land (or, hell, the ocean or space, if you insisted) and chucking it there, and guarding it. Retrieve it with a drone whenever you need it, since you don't need to worry about wear and tear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Safe comes in two flavors. The most common form is that you need to do something for it to do something.

This is an uncommon form; it acts on its own, but the action is extremely predictable, follows a specific routine, and otherwise requires minimal conditions for containment. You can pretty much set your watch to this thing. The once-a-month hanging requirement, the worst thing about it, is easily solved by directing a D-class to the noose instead of the termination chamber once a month (if that's your headcanon.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this Safe and not Euclid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think that hanging people ever should be "easy". It fails the locked box test for safe anyway. You can't just lock it up and forget about it. IMO, it is solidly Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I WANT to upvote this for potential, but it falls so short that I considered downvoting before reminding myself it isn't bad, just disappointing. No vote.
PLEASE do more with this. It nearly hits my theological horror buttons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this felt like it was cut short. I quite like short and sweet, but here the narrative seems just "there". Sure, there's an implied narrative of its backstory, but it's not very distinctive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think a JSA comic had someone actually asked that question, though I can't find it off-hand.

Presumably, Foundation personnel certainly believe that things like THE DEER exist, but aren't actually divine, and religion-related SCPs are just taking aspects of a religion, in the same way that they might believe that 2137 isn't actually Tupac Shakur, just using his voice and music.

They might also believe that there must be more qualifications to being a 'God' than anything the Foundation could interact with. If God is Supernatural, the Foundation can only work with things that are natural - Anomalous, but natural. If we can contain THE DEER, regardless of how difficult and complex it is to do, and how unsure we are about it, is it truly divine?

What about monotheistic things? We've seen stuff that might imply that the Christian God exists, but we've also seen stuff of Hindu Gods, and this noose thing… How's a guy to choose? Unless the canon of their foundation only includes SCPs related to one religion, none of this is exactly persuasive.

They could also be people who make a distinction between belief in existence, and worship. They don't doubt these things exist, but see no reason to worship them, which they (might) see as necessary for a god. For example, were I a foundation scientist, even one assigned to worth with THE DEER, I wouldn't doubt it existed. The foundation gods might be far more powerful than us, but they're still not gods. I think John Cena exists, has more power and influence than I do, and can kick my as… But don't worship him.

While an atheist working with the foundation might express some different views from one outside the foundation, I'm sure religious people within the foundation would as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just really don't see why in the world the Foundation would bother feeding the thing. Just stuff it somewhere quiet and it doesn't do anything harmful at all, does it? Even if it starts screaming loudly in people's minds, there's no real reason to waste valuable resources on it - the Foundation doesn't have infinite D-Class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think they mean the view point of the gods being hanged. Which is more than likely with how the world is today, with people being filled with greed, pride, envy, and brutality toward each other and toward the beliefs of others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A small question I have would be how many atheists can exist within the foundation when they work with numerous literal gods, possible gods and items that work in specifically religious ways.

Similar to the common question of how certain comic book superheros could be atheist when they regularly fight with and against gods.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the fact that it wasnt big was the point though. And that's why it demands this stuff. To feel big.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel there is unreleased potential here. For one thing, I think an example of the "interviews" with the hanged would give more atmosphere. It could also be a way to hide an explanation on how the hanged communicate.

Secondly 3.7 minutes? Wouldn't "3 minutes 42 seconds" be a better way to describe it. Using decimals for time periods more detailed that quarters of an hour was something I learned in University was to be avoided.

No vote from me, yet

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something caught my eye, I hope it's okay to point it out.

Qualified subjects hanged using SCP-2121 will remain conscious for an average of 3.7 minutes longer than expected by use of a non-anomalous noose and remain capable of speech for the entire duration prior to loss of consciousness.

By definition, if you're talking, you're breathing. So, unless giving people the power to speak without air flowing into and out of their lungs is some part of the object's anomalous powers (which may be your intention) it might be something you want to modify. As it stands, it reads to me like the person goes from breathing and talking just fine to suddenly dead without any actual suffocation in between.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And now I have the mental image of this being a byproduct of a collaborative effort between Vecna and Nerull from D&D trying to get drunk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my god, I thought the main page said god's nose… I was so confused…

item.title: Re: Underdeveloped
item.content:encoded: I can't say for any of the other points, but I don't think the Foundation would ever try to starve it to death or destroy it unless it posed some massive threat that they could not hope to reliably contain forever (e.g. SCP-682).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. It's a solid object. Not a whole lot more to be said - not every object needs to be something really mind blowing.

I wouldn't mind perhaps a little bit more information about the backstory of the noose, perhaps in a document or interview or some such, but it would have to be very well written, otherwise it might end up just coming across as filler.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There needs to be some more testing done on this.
Also, why throw class D personel at it, when you could just isolate it? I'm sure The Foundation could just set up a perimiter around it's effective range, far more effective than wasting human D class lives. Or maybe if you do not feed it, its range will increase? Maybe they're afraid it will worsen if left "unfed", and that's why they do it in the first place?
Great potential. Other than that, I like it.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not really. My take on S/E/K is about ease of containment. Yes, it requires a human sacrifice monthly, but it's very simple to do that sacrifice, and it doesn't have any other passive effects that would cause containment breaches. Take a look at my SCP-1979: it requires someone to be constantly using it or it will destroy the world in roughly 12 hours. But using it is easy. Same thing here: if there was some ultracomplicated ritual where they had to shave the sacrifice and then tattoo a mystic pattern while pouring pigshit over them while they died… then it'd be Euclid, due to the complexity of containment measures. This is just easy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're right. For some reason, I was certain that the Russian Orthodox Church was a Catholic off-shoot, recognized as part of Catholicism. I've made corrections.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If proper containment requires a monthly human sacrifice, I'd say this thing is at the very least Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it was doing that I should be feeling some dread because of the scope. I ain't feeling dread, and I ain't feeling scope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it could use more, but it makes me feel…unsettled. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This, plus the religious stuff just feels shoehorned in. It doesn't really add anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd say even a small god is a credible threat, not to mention the possibility that this thing is more insidious than it seems.

Mayhaps it does not need sustenance, and the whole point of this thing is to ensure that a sufficiently large area would remain clear of "traditional" religious presence for a sufficiently long time.

Maybe it's not feeding itself.

Maybe it's not trying to exact vengeance.

Maybe it's trying to establish a beachhead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Russian Orthodox Catholic

What? Are you talking about Eastern catholics? 'cause I'm pretty sure that it's called the Uniate or just Russian Catholic Church. But yeah, I was ready to upvote, and then it just kind of… ended. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Impractical simmering spite is all fine and good, but it doesn't work when I'm apparently supposed to take this thing as a legitimate threat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But it doesn't really convey any meaningful spite in any way. Or meaningless spite. You get the idea. It's almost like a small child, wailing for food. I agree with Djoric here. Gods should be BIG.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno.

I dig the somewhat impractical simmering spite this thing conveys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you want a Russian-ish religious figure that would maintain strong ties with Catholicism while self-identifying as Orthodox, I would suggest an Orthodox ecumenist, what a "hardore" orthodox would call "mirolog-ecumenist" (do note that "mirolog" has become almost offensive).
Do note that mainstream Russian Orthodoxy considers ecumenism to be a form of heresy (though nowaday it rarely defrocks priests who demonstrate ecumenist leanings).

Would be a nice twist if the priest developed ecumenistic leanings based on the fact that the rope makes no distinctions between different religions.

BTW, would the rope affect people of more exotic beliefs?
Would it "get" Panentheists or shamanists of some sort (the latter still believe in higher spirits, which might be "close enough" to a god for the effect to "stick"…)
Requesting test log ;-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoops. Misread "non-Catholic" as "catholic", mea culpa.

However, my first point, the one regarding, and here I copypaste:

The local Russian Orthodox Catholic priest was found

still stands.

I might be misunderstanding something, but as far as I know Catholicism and Russian Orthodoxy are mutually exclusive beliefs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted.

It's not big enough.

If a god is to be established as a significant power, there needs to be a feeling of bigness. Tophet is big. 1348 is big. THE DEER is big. The Scarlet King is huge. When I read this, I get the feeling that this is just some piddling local god, and with that in mind I can't take it very seriously at all: it feels far more appropriate that the Initiative would starve it out of hiding and murder the shit out of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure where you got "Catholic tribes" from… the extermination was of tribal peoples that weren't Catholic. I.e. "pagans"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Boring. This is an indestructible sausage rope that murders you. There's nothing more to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was about do downvote this, when I realized that I my stomach had started tingling out of unnervedness. Good job Drewbear. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like it was said before, you could do more with this. Other than that, I quite liked this. I actually had a thought of trying to think up an SCP that was an execution device (gallows, guillitine, etc.), but never got around to it. Guess you beat me to the punch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and a single 1.3 meter tongue tied

That was on purpose and you know it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well in my defense, I haven't read the thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While there are Catholic groups in Russia (both rural and metropolitan), to the best of my knowledge they aren't considered (and don't consider themselves to be) tribes ^_~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but I suppose I'm a mite biased. Nonetheless, good job, Drewbear!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A Catholic tribe is a tribe that practices Catholicism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait a moment, Russian Orthodox Catholic.. how does that work ?
Also, what is a "Catholic tribe" ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article has very strong imagery, which is a big plus. It's good enough as-is to make me upvote.

I'd love to see some elaboration, though, as others have said.

item.title: Underdeveloped
item.content:encoded:
So it's a god - jealous of other gods and demanding sacrifices.

  • What happens if you try to feed it animals? Or give it already dead people?
  • What happens if you just let it go hungry? If it's biological and needs to eat flesh to survive, why hasn't the foundation tried starving it to death?
  • Does praying to it have any effect?
  • You say interviews are allowed with D-Class personnel while hanging. Could you transcribe some of them? Do they feel blessed? Happy? Do they glimpse heaven or hell?
  • Can it assimilate an atheist?

There's a lot of tests the foundation could run, and a lot of possible backstory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I more or less agree with Roget above, but you managed to skeeve me out so bad by a tongue noose that I couldn't not upvote. I would love to see a deeper narrative or maybe something better than scary screams + feed it D-class though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a little curious as to why the Foundation is feeding this thing D-class personnel honestly. Sure it gets loud, but considering the area of effect, sticking it in an isolated site/area would work reasonably well.

I can _sort_ of see the implications you're trying to get at via the last paragraph - but I think it's a little too obscured/subtle. The narrative around what exactly the victims are saying is a also a little muddled.

Vocalizations are exclusively of a religious nature and include wails of shock and despair over the apparent deaths of a variety of divine figures, curses against and insults towards an unidentified and unnamed deific figure, and pleas of mercy towards the same figure. Epithets used to describe the unknown deific figure indicate a tendency towards jealousy and violent retribution

Are the vocalizations from the point of view of the victim (IE are they still themselves?). Are the from the point of view of people who died before? Or are they from the point of view of the dying gods themselves? It's just not clear to me from what's written here.

The effect could use a little more work too - it's well written, but it's more or less 'crazy to death' when you get right down to it.

No vote for now.
